body_txt: Stereotypically, it's good for a female character to be feminine. A girly girl is kind, gentle, polite, and doesn't put herself forward; a tomboy is rude, loud, 'mannish,' and domineering. But sometimes the assumptions are reversed: the delicate flower is a manipulative bitch, while the independent tomboy has the drive and means to look out for everybody, not just herself. This trope is quite common in stories set in high school where the Alpha Bitch is the prettiest, most feminine girl in school, but the heroine is too busy doing track and shop class to worry about her manicure. It also can be found in Action Girl -centric media, where the heroine is right in the center of the fight, while the villainess is a sheltered aristocrat , an evil queen , or a Dark Magical Girl . Obviously, a Sub-Trope of Tomboy and Girly Girl . Compare Real Women Don't Wear Dresses . See also, Nothing Nice About Sugar and Spice , Slobs Versus Snobs , and Makeup Is Evil . May overlap with Screw the Rules, I'm Beautiful! ; Wasted Beauty ; Polite Villains, Rude Heroes ; True Beauty Is on the Inside ; Ugly Hero, Good-Looking Villain (if tomboyish girl is Hollywood Homely ); Shabby Heroes, Well-Dressed Villains ; and Privileged Rival . For a tamer and less specific variant, see Light Feminine and Dark Feminine , where it doesn't require morality.     Danny Phantom Luca Randy Cunningham: 9th Grade Ninja Total Drama
